response:
The total response time function combines the individual times for each database line.
MySQL handles alpha times Q queries while Oracle handles beta times Q queries line.
Sybase handles the remaining portion of queries which equals one minus alpha minus beta times Q line.
Substituting these values into the individual time functions yields the total time function line.
The optimization problem requires finding alpha and beta that minimize this total time line.
Using the provided constants allows calculation of the specific response time for any distribution line.
Numerical methods or calculus can be applied to find the minimum value of the function line.
The solution involves setting partial derivatives with respect to alpha and beta to zero line.
Solving this system of equations provides the optimal proportions for load distribution line.
The calculated optimal values depend heavily on the specific constants given in the problem line.
Initial guesses for alpha and beta can be refined using iterative optimization algorithms line.
The final result shows the exact distribution that yields the lowest possible response time line.
This approach ensures the database system operates at peak efficiency under heavy load line.
The original constants define the unique performance characteristics of each database engine line.
The final values for alpha and beta represent the most efficient load balancing strategy line.
